---
title: "About Me"
image: /images/profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mdastgheib/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/mohdasti
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: /CV/CV_MDastgheib.pdf
    - icon: ai-google-scholar
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=SNVpHcUAAAAJ
format:
  html:
    output-file: index.html
    css:
      - ../assets/css/research-skills.css
---

I am a cognitive neuroscientist and human factors researcher focused on a core question: why does performance degrade under pressure, and where in the decision process does it happen? My work sits at the intersection of experimental psychology and applied human factors, using multi-modal methods to study how physical effort, arousal, and aging shape perception and memory.

The central tool is a handgrip dual-task paradigm—concurrent isometric grip with auditory and visual discrimination tasks—combined with pupillometry and hierarchical Bayesian drift-diffusion modeling. The goal is not a single headline metric, but an interpretable decomposition of dual-task costs that can inform the design of adaptive systems, interfaces, and human factors evaluations.

---

## Education

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
![](/images/ucr.png){fig-alt="University of California, Riverside"}
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### PhD in Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience
[University of California, Riverside]{.edu-org}

[2021 — 2026 (Expected)]{.edu-years}
:::
:::

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
![](/images/queensu.png){fig-alt="Queen's University"}
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### M.Sc. in Psychology
[Queen's University]{.edu-org}

[2017 — 2020]{.edu-years}
:::
:::

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
![](/images/yorku.png){fig-alt="York University"}
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### Hon. B.Sc. in Biological Sciences & Psychology
[York University]{.edu-org}

[2013 — 2017]{.edu-years}
:::
:::

## Industry Experience

::: {.edu-entry}
::: {.edu-logo}
![](/images/sst.jpeg){fig-alt="Surgical Safety Technologies"}
:::
::: {.edu-body}
### Research Assistant I
[Surgical Safety Technologies]{.edu-org}

[Toronto, ON — 2021]{.edu-years}
:::
:::
